audit_status.source_base: Greek Jude text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Complete one-chapter draft reviewed for contending for trust, false teachers, wilderness/angel/Sodom warnings, Michael tradition, Enoch citation, mercy, doxology. Relation to 2 Peter and non-canonical citations require second-pass audit.

verses.restored: Jude, servant of Jesus Messiah and sibling of James, to those called, beloved in Divine Father and kept for Jesus Messiah:
Mercy to you and peace and love be multiplied.
Beloved, making all eagerness to write to you concerning our common salvation/healing, I had necessity to write appealing to you to contend for the trust once handed over to the holy ones.

For certain humans slipped in unnoticed, those long ago written beforehand for this judgment, irreverent ones turning the grace of our Divine into licentiousness and denying our only Master and Lord, Jesus Messiah.
Now I wish to remind you, though you know all, that the Lord, having saved/healed a people out of Egypt, afterward destroyed those not trusting.
And messengers who did not keep their own beginning/office but left their own dwelling, he has kept in age-long bonds under gloom for judgment of great day.

As Sodom and Gomorrah and the surrounding cities, in like manner giving themselves over to sexual immorality/misuse and going after other flesh, are set forth as example, undergoing justice of age-long fire.
Yet likewise these dreamers also defile flesh, reject lordship, and blaspheme glories.
But Michael the archangel, when disputing with the adversarial one, was reasoning about the body of Moses, did not dare bring blasphemous judgment, but said: May Lord rebuke you.
But these blaspheme what they do not know; and what they understand naturally like irrational living creatures, by these they are corrupted.
Woe to them, because they went in the way of Cain, and poured themselves into the error of Balaam for wages, and perished in the rebellion of Korah.
These are hidden reefs in your love-feasts, feasting with you fearlessly, shepherding themselves, waterless clouds carried along by winds, autumn trees without fruit, twice dead, uprooted,
wild waves of sea foaming up their own shames, wandering stars, for whom gloom of darkness has been kept into the age.
And Enoch, seventh from Adam, also prophesied to these, saying: Look, Lord came with his holy myriads,

to make judgment concerning all and to expose every soul/life concerning all their works of irreverence that they irreverently did, and concerning all the hard things that irreverent sinners/misaligned ones spoke against him.
These are grumblers, fault-finders, going according to their desires, and their mouth speaks swollen things, admiring faces for advantage.
But you, beloved, remember the words spoken beforehand by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Messiah,
that they were saying to you: In last time there will be mockers going according to their own irreverent desires.
These are the ones making divisions, soul-level, not having Spirit.
But you, beloved, building yourselves up in your most holy trust, praying in holy Spirit,
keep yourselves in love of Divine, awaiting the mercy of our Lord Jesus Messiah into age-long life.
And have mercy on those doubting,
save/heal others by snatching from fire; and on others have mercy with reverent caution, hating even the garment stained by flesh.
Now to the One able to guard you unstumbling and to stand you before his glory blameless in exultation,
to only Divine our Savior/Healer through Jesus Messiah our Lord be glory, majesty, strength, and authority before all the age and now and into all the ages. Amen.

safety_note: Jude contains severe polemic and apocalyptic imagery. Do not use it to dehumanize opponents, demonize ordinary disagreement, or target vulnerable groups. The polemic addresses exploitative, divisive, self-serving teachers who distort grace and harm the community. Sodom/Gomorrah and 'other flesh' require second-pass source and reception-history audit to avoid later prooftext misuse.
translation_notes: Jude uses traditions also known from 1 Enoch and the Assumption/Testament of Moses stream; non-canonical citation metadata required in second pass.
Mercy remains central in vv. 21-23 even amid severe warnings.
Doxology centers the Divine's ability to guard from stumbling.
